title: Drone design website MVP

requirements.text: Create a responsive single-page website for designing drones, with clear navigation between a landing page and a drone design workspace.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: App has at least two routes/pages: a landing page and a design workspace.
Navigation between pages is obvious on desktop and mobile.
No placeholder lorem-ipsum remains in user-facing text.

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity sign-in and show signed-in state in the UI so users can save and load their drone designs under their identity.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: User can sign in/out with Internet Identity.
UI clearly indicates whether the user is signed in.
Saving/loading designs is disabled (with a helpful message) when signed out.

requirements.text: Provide a drone design editor that lets users configure a drone by selecting common components (at minimum: frame size/type, motor, propeller, battery, flight controller, camera/optional payload) and adjusting key parameters (e.g., quantity, KV, prop size, battery cell count).

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: Workspace includes a component list and an editor UI to change component selections/parameters.
User can add/remove or change at least the minimum component categories listed.
The current design state updates immediately in the UI when edits are made.

requirements.text: Render a live 3D preview of the configured drone in the workspace using Three.js / React Three Fiber, with basic orbit controls and clear visual differentiation between major parts (frame, motors, props, battery, camera/payload).

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: 3D preview renders in the workspace without errors.
User can rotate/zoom the view via orbit controls.
Changing components in the editor visibly changes the 3D preview (at least for frame size/type and prop size).

requirements.text: Calculate and display a simple design summary for the current configuration (at minimum: estimated all-up weight, estimated total thrust, thrust-to-weight ratio, and an estimated flight time range) using transparent, user-visible assumptions.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: Summary panel shows the required metrics.
Assumptions/inputs used for calculations are visible (e.g., per-motor thrust estimate, battery capacity, efficiency factor).
Metrics update when relevant component parameters change.

requirements.text: Implement backend persistence for drone designs: create, list, load, update, and delete designs per authenticated user, including timestamps and a user-provided name for each design.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes methods to create, list, get, update, and delete designs for the calling principal.
Design records include: id, name, configuration payload, createdAt, updatedAt.
A user can only access their own saved designs.

requirements.text: Add a “My Designs” panel in the UI that integrates with the backend (via React Query) to save the current design, view saved designs, load a design into the editor, rename it, and delete it.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: User can save the current design with a name.
Saved designs list shows at least name and last updated time.
User can load a saved design and see the editor + 3D preview update accordingly.
User can rename and delete a saved design.
UI shows loading and error states for all backend actions.

requirements.text: Provide a coherent visual theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, and component styling) appropriate for a drone design tool and apply it consistently across all pages.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: Landing page and workspace share a consistent visual system (type scale, spacing, buttons, panels).
Theme avoids default/unstyled appearance; UI looks intentional and tool-like.
Theme does not rely on blue/purple as the primary palette.

requirements.text: Add static generated brand/hero imagery and use it on the landing page and in the app shell (e.g., header) without serving images through the backend.

quotes: I'd like a website for designing drones.

acceptanceCriteria: Generated images are placed under frontend/public/assets/generated and referenced from the UI.
Landing page includes the hero image.
App includes a logo mark in the header or equivalent app chrome.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor with all logic in the main actor file; only add migration code if an upgrade requires it.

Do not edit frontend immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui (compose these instead).
Use English for all user-facing text.
nonGoals: Exporting CAD/STL/STEP files or integrating with external CAD tools.
Integrations with external drone part marketplaces or third-party APIs.
Advanced physics simulation, PID tuning, or firmware flashing workflows.

required: Modern geometric drone wireframe hero illustration on a dark neutral background (drone-hero.dim_1600x900.png)
